full_text: Wheal to Scholtz, 2 runs, back of a length and at the stumps, Scholtz backs away and hammers the pull through mid-wicket, scampers back for the second even as

short_text: Wheal to Scholtz, 2 runs, banged in short and at the stumps, Scholtz chops the pull through mid-wicket and scampers back
full_text: Wheal to Scholtz, 2 runs, banged in short and at the stumps, Scholtz chops the pull through mid-wicket and scampers back for the second - they're checking for a short run, Scholtz did plonk his bat onto the crease, but it got stuck, but he's managed to get it over the line

short_text: Wheal to Smit, Smit Run Out!! 1 run completed. Smit loses his wicket in the quest to keep strike - was punched down towa
full_text: Wheal to Smit, Smit Run Out!! 1 run completed. Smit loses his wicket in the quest to keep strike - was punched down towards long-on and there was never a second, but Smit wants it and turns blind, his partner ain't going anywhere, the return is accurate and Wheal breaks the stumps, finding him well short. Could Scholtz have sacrificed himself?

full_text: Wheal to Scholtz, 1 run, fuller length delivery on off, driven back at the bowler who can only palm it towards mid-off, the single brings up Namibia's 150

short_text: Wheal to Smit, , , Smit is a powerful man and he'll need to end this innings on a high - back of a length and at the stu
full_text: Wheal to Smit, , , Smit is a powerful man and he'll need to end this innings on a high - back of a length and at the stumps, he picked the length early and then shuffled into the pull, hit with the wind and went sailing over wide long-on

short_text: Bradley Currie to Trumpelmann, Caught by Wheal!! Another catch in the deep and it's falling apart for Namibia here, leng
full_text: Bradley Currie to Trumpelmann, Caught by Wheal!! Another catch in the deep and it's falling apart for Namibia here, length ball on the pads and Trumpelmann played the pick-up shovel, but it went high and it went against the wind, it meant that there was no distance - Wheal gets under it at deep backward square and completes a calm catch.

short_text: Wheal to Zane Green, Caught by Michael Jones!! Wheal has three now, Nambia losing the plot towards the end. on a length
full_text: Wheal to Zane Green, Caught by Michael Jones!! Wheal has three now, Nambia losing the plot towards the end. on a length and just outside off - Green came down the pitch, made room and then failed to get elevation on the drive, chipped it uppishly and straight to the fielder at mid-off - the plan was right, but the execution went awry.

short_text: Wheal to Wiese, Caught by Mark Watt!! A wild swing and Wiese holes out! This is a massive wicket in the context of the i
full_text: Wheal to Wiese, Caught by Mark Watt!! A wild swing and Wiese holes out! This is a massive wicket in the context of the innings - the slower ball doing the trick for Wheal, rolled his fingers across the seam, Wiese's early into the slog, goes through with the shot and the thick edge lobs over backward point - Watt settles under and holds on.

short_text: Leask to Erasmus, Stumped!! Erasmus has given it away now - a pre-empted charge down the pitch, Leask saw it coming and
full_text: Leask to Erasmus, Stumped!! Erasmus has given it away now - a pre-empted charge down the pitch, Leask saw it coming and dragged the length back, Erasmus's is beaten in the flight, but he still goes through with the slog and misses - Cross with the easiest of stumpings, finding the Namibian captain well short. A fine knock, but they'd have wanted their captain to bat deep.

full_text: Leask to Erasmus, , , tad too full from Leask - right in Erasmus's zone, he's picked the length early, bends down  low and then swipes it wide of long-on, clears the ropes on the full for a maximum, brings his his

full_text: Greaves to Malan Kruger, Caught by Michael Jones!! That's an excellent catch and Namibia have lost four now. Was a well flighted delivery outside off - Kruger came down the pitch, didn't quite get to the pitch and then had to drag the lofted drive, went to long-on's right where Jones ran across and completed a good running catch, right in front of the ropes.

full_text: Christopher Sole to Davin, Caught by Bradley Currie!! full_text: Christopher Sole to Davin, , , fuller length delivery outside off, Davin throws his hands on the drive, gets a thick edge that went flying through slip for a boundary

full_text: Bradley Currie to Davin, 1 run, , but Sole can't hold on - short of a good length and just outside off, Davin didn't get the elevation behind the pull, went to the right of Sole who sprinted across mid-on, threw himself with a one-handed effort and the ball doesn't stick

full_text: Bradley Currie to Davin, no run, on a good length and around off, pushed back at the bowler

full_text: Bradley Currie to Erasmus, 1 run, fuller length short of length outside off, driven to the left of mid-off for a single

short_text: Bradley Currie to Jan Frylinck, Bowled!! Dragged on! Currie is off on a celebratory run - short of a good length and hol
full_text: Bradley Currie to Jan Frylinck, Bowled!! Dragged on! Currie is off on a celebratory run - short of a good length and holding its line outside off, Frylinck was shaping to heave it onto the on-side, was through with the shot early and dragged a big inside edge, clattering into the middle-stump.

full_text: Bradley Currie to Jan Frylinck, no run, on a good length and well wide of off, Frylinck goes for an ambitious drive and misses

full_text: Wheal to Jan Frylinck, , nicely played, on a good length and angling across the left-hander - Frylinck makes room and then finds the gap square past cover-point, long chase for the fielder and he comes second best, despite the dive

full_text: Wheal to Jan Frylinck, , that's serious pace, Cross has to leap and he can just palm it over, landed very close to the helmet and is called as a wide for height - banged in short and it kept rising onto the left-hander

full_text: Wheal to Jan Frylinck, , , on a good length and in that channel just outside off, the line drew Frylinck into the push, gets a thick edge and the ball flies wide of the lone slip - third man was inside the ring and has no chance

full_text: Wheal to Jan Frylinck, no run, half-an-appeal for LBW, but the ball has pitched outside leg, Frylinck was looking to flick and missed

short_text: Wheal to Kotze, Caught by McMullen!! Caught at short mid-wicket, Scotland have struck early, short of a good length and
full_text: Wheal to Kotze, Caught by McMullen!! Caught at short mid-wicket, Scotland have struck early, short of a good length and angling across the left-hander, wasn't too short for the pull - Kotze stayed on the front-foot as he went for the big shot, hit high on the bat and he mistimed a dolly to mid-wicket - McMullen with the catch.

short_text: Wheal to Kotze, no run, fuller length delivery on off, Kotze gets well forward and finds mid-off with the drive
full_text: Wheal to Kotze, no run, fuller length delivery on off, Kotze gets well forward and finds mid-off with the drive

full_text: Wheal to Kotze, no run, shout for LBW, but this was swinging down leg and , an inswinging full-toss and Kotze was beaten on the flick, struck low on the pad and in front of leg

full_text: Wiese to Berrington, , Berrington ends it in style. A slower ball off-cutter outside off, Berrington gets forward and thumps it way back over wide long-on, flies flat

full_text: Wiese to Greaves, 1 run, Greaves shuffles across and gets a slight tickle on the flick, Zane Green dives to his left and saves more runs, but they take one

full_text: Wiese to Greaves, 2 runs, slower ball off-cutter outside off, pulled in the air and mid-wicket dives forward but fails to stop it, gives away two

short_text: Trumpelmann to Leask, Caught by Wiese!! End of Leask but that should be the match-winning innings. A slower ball full ou
full_text: Trumpelmann to Leask, Caught by Wiese!! End of Leask but that should be the match-winning innings. A slower ball full outside off, Leask swipes but finds long-on. Wiese moves across to pouch it safely low down.

full_text: Trumpelmann to Berrington, 1 run, Berrington comes down the track and takes it on the full, just about gets it over the outstretched left hand of Trumpelmann, one more

full_text: Trumpelmann to Leask, , 2 runs, it gets worse for Namibia. A low full-toss outside off, toe-ended straight down the ground for two and a front foot no-ball

full_text: Trumpelmann to Leask, , what a blow that is. 102m hit into the wind at deep mid-wicket. A full delivery outside off, a golf swing from Leask and the white ball soars. What an innings this is from Leask

full_text: Trumpelmann to Berrington, 1 run, full on off, lofted over extra-cover and just clears the fielder

full_text: Wiese to Leask, , poor poor delivery and that has gone into the top tier at deep square leg. A low full-toss on the stumps, smashed by Leask and it has gone a long way. 19 off the over and the equation has become easier for Scotland

full_text: Scholtz to Cross, Lbw!! That's so plumb that Cross almost walked for that. Full on the stumps, bang on middle, Cross misses the slogsweep and is hit on the back leg. Easy for umpire Nitin Menon who raises the finger. Cross has a word with Berrington about the review but the time runs out by then. Namibia on top.

short_text: Erasmus to McMullen, Stumped!! That's very sloppy from McMullen. Misses the drive to a length delivery, Zane Green fumbl
full_text: Erasmus to McMullen, Stumped!! That's very sloppy from McMullen. Misses the drive to a length delivery, Zane Green fumbles and the ball rolls close to the stumps, Zane Green spots that McMullen is dozy and whips the bails off. McMullen's back foot was outside the crease and in the air, the heel did not get back in. He had all the time in the world to get back, but he was holding the pose and did not know where the heel was, it was in the air and not down on the ground. Poor cricket that.

short_text: Erasmus to Berrington, , fired down leg, Berrington gets low to sweep, misses, Zane Green misses too, called wides and E
full_text: Erasmus to Berrington, , fired down leg, Berrington gets low to sweep, misses, Zane Green misses too, called wides and Erasmus is not happy

full_text: Erasmus to Berrington, no run, angled across Berrington on a good length who goes for a big hit on the off-side, misses

full_text: Erasmus to McMullen, 1 run, Dabbed to the left of backward point and a slight fumble, but the throw goes to Zane Green and wide of him, had it gone to Erasmus' end, it would have been a great chance of a run-out. Both batters were halfway down, Erasmus goes down theatrically on his back as the throw does not come to him

full_text: Scholtz to Berrington, no run, drifts in and Berrington gets a thick inside-edge on the forward defence, the ball rolls behind his back

short_text: Lungameni to Michael Jones, , magnificent shot. Michael Jones comes down the track and makes room to give the room to ex
full_text: Lungameni to Michael Jones, , magnificent shot. Michael Jones comes down the track and makes room to give the room to extend his arms, lofts over extra-cover and it flies all the way

short_text: Lungameni to Michael Jones, , Michael Jones gets lucky, a short of length delivery on off, he pulls and the thick inside
full_text: Lungameni to Michael Jones, , Michael Jones gets lucky, a short of length delivery on off, he pulls and the thick inside-edge runs just wide of the stumps and to the fine-leg fence

short_text: Lungameni to McMullen, 3 runs, a thick outside-edge as McMullen gets forward to push, wide of point and they run hard to
full_text: Lungameni to McMullen, 3 runs, a thick outside-edge as McMullen gets forward to push, wide of point and they run hard to come back for three, Michael Jones was the one who initiated the third

full_text: Lungameni to Michael Jones, , full and in the slot outside off, Michael Jones smashes the loft straight down the ground, no holding back

full_text: Wiese to McMullen, no run, slower ball on a good length outside off, McMullen is deceived, pushes and is beaten, was the knuckle-ball

full_text: Lungameni to Munsey, Caught by Wiese!! Good low catch at mid-off. End of a scratchy knock. Munsey tries to loft on the rise, does not get hold of it, Wiese judges it well and dives forward to take a nice catch.

full_text: Trumpelmann to Munsey, no run, good diving stop by Frylinck at cover, forces the batters to stay at their ends. Munsey walks into the shot and drives

full_text: Trumpelmann to Munsey, , Munsey gives the charge and that forces Trumpelmann to follow him, but it's outside the leg-stump and no chance for Zane Green to his right. Five bonus runs
